data.title: Bitcoin reaches all-time high of $100,000 on optimism over Trump's SEC pick
data.description:
Bitcoin

-
  • -
  • -
  • Volume: -
Open Trading A/c
-
0 (0%)
Todays L/H
0
0

Bitcoin prices have reached an all-time high of $100,000, after a brief pause seen in the recent rally, that has been powered by the anticipation that Trump 2.0 administration will be friendlier to the digital asset.

Pro-Crypto SEC Pick

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Trump has picked Paul Atkins to succeed outgoing SEC Chair Gary Gensler, who had implemented strict regulations on digital assets. Donald Trump has promised to appoint regulators more agreeable to the digital asset industry, and has plans for a strategic stockpile of Bitcoin. The recently concluded US election also saw candidates supportive of cryptocurrencies emerge as lawmakers.

Putin Backs Cryptos

Some Bitcoin participants also attributed the comments from Russian President Vladimir Putin to the rally. Putin had said at an economic forum in Moscow that nobody can prohibit the use of Bitcoin and other virtual currencies.

ETF Money Soaring

US Exchange-Traded Funds investing in Bitcoins have seen a net inflow of about $32 billion this year, with over $8 billion coming in since Trump's election victory, data from Bloomberg shows.

The Rise of Crypto

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The market capitalization of the crypto asset is now just shy of $2 trillion level. To put this in context, Nvidia and Apple's market capitalisation is just over $3.5 trillion and $3.6 trillion respectively.

Bitcoin Flirted With $100,000 for Past Two Weeks. Chart source: Bloomberg News

The market capitalization of all the gold in the world is roughly $17.7 trillion.

The enthusiasm around bitcoin is expected to continue, especially in anticipation of supportive policies from the incoming Trump administration, which is encouraging a favourable environment for the digital asset.

Trump has promised to undo a clampdown on digital assets, and has even backed a strategic national Bitcoin reserve.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Musk's xAI plans massive expansion of AI supercomputer in Memphis
data.description:
As part of the expansion, Nvidia, which supplies the GPUs, and Dell and Super Micro, which have assembled the server racks for the computer, will establish operations in Memphis, the chamber said in a statement. Reuters

Elon Musk's artificial intelligence startup xAI plans to expand its Memphis, Tennessee, supercomputer to house at least one million graphics processing units (GPUs), the Greater Memphis Chamber said on Wednesday, as xAI races to compete against rivals like OpenAI.

The move represents a massive expansion for the supercomputer called Colossus, which currently has 100,000 GPUs to train xAI's chatbot called Grok.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

As part of the expansion, Nvidia, which supplies the GPUs, and Dell and Super Micro, which have assembled the server racks for the computer, will establish operations in Memphis, the chamber said in a statement.

Colossus is a critical component of advancing Musk's AI efforts, as the billionaire has deepened his rivalry against OpenAI and its chief executive Sam Altman. Last month, Musk expanded his lawsuit against the ChatGPT-maker, arguing that OpenAI and Microsoft illegally sought to monopolize the AI market.

The plan to expand the xAI supercomputer tenfold will likely raise environmental concerns, as activists and lawmakers have scrutinized the growing number of data centers for their vast energy consumption. In August, the Southern Environmental Law Center called on local Tennessee officials to verify whether xAI was using gas turbines to help power its supercomputer without the required air permits.

data.title: PG Electroplast opens QIP to raise up to Rs 1,500 crore, floor price Rs 705 per share
data.description:
PG Electroplast

  • Watchlist

  • Set Alert
 
 
live

  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Diversified plastic moulding and design company PG Electroplast has initiated an institutional placement of shares to QIBs at a floor price of Rs 705.18 per share, the company said on December 4, as it aims to raise up to Rs 1,500 crore.

The QIP will lead to an equity dilution of 6.7% stake, and the proceeds will be used for working capital requirement, loan repayment or other general corporate purposes. PG Electroplast had taken the board's approval on October 19, and shareholders' nod for the share placement through a special resolution on November 13.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

CNBC-TV18 quoted sources who suggested that the indicative issue price of the QIP is at Rs 690-699 per share.

The management during the earnings call in November that it is seeing some 'very significant growth opportunities' coming its way. "...if as and when they kind of materialize, we will be utilizing that money for capex and working capital."

This is being updated

data.title: World Street | US benchmarks hit record highs, French govt topples, American Eagle's bleak outlook and more
data.description:
World Street offers a sneak peek into the world of business and economy.

The three US benchmarks--the Dow Jones Industrial Average, the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite--all hit record highs overnight on the back of a surge in tech stocks. Apparel retailer American Eagle Outfitters slashed its full-year sales guidance and rolled out a bleak holiday outlook. Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey hinted at the possibility of four interest rate cuts over the coming year if inflation continues to ease out. The French government has been overthrown following a vote of no confidence. All this and more in the December 5 edition of World Street.

Party on the Street

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The S&P 500, Dow Jones Industrial Average and Nasdaq Composite soared to record highs on Wednesday, driven by a rally in tech stocks following robust earnings reports from Salesforce and Marvell Technology. All three major indices reached all-time intraday highs during the session and closed at record levels, with the Dow surpassing the 45,000 mark for the first time.

The market move come at a time when investors turned their attention to the upcoming US employment data scheduled for release on Friday. Economists surveyed by Dow Jones anticipate the US economy added 214,000 jobs in November. Meanwhile, a report from ADP on Wednesday showed private payroll growth fell short of expectations for the month.

Darkness beholds

American Eagle shares tumbled 13% in extended trading on Wednesday after the company reported mixed third-quarter earnings, rolled out a weak holiday guidance and lowered its full-year forecast. The company attributed the challenges to value-conscious consumers focused on spending during key shopping events.

While American Eagle narrowly missed Wall Street's revenue expectations, it exceeded earnings projections. This marks the third consecutive quarter in which American Eagle has failed to meet Wall Street's sales targets, reflecting ongoing pressures in the consumption space.

For the holiday quarter, the company expects comparable sales to rise 1%, but total sales to drop 4%, partly due to the calendar shift. This outlook lags the 1% sales decline anticipated by LSEG. Full-year guidance has been revised, with comparable sales now expected to grow 3% instead of 4%, and total sales projected to increase by just 1%, down from the earlier range of 2% to 3%.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Something to celebrate

Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey hinted on Wednesday that the UK might see up to four interest rate cuts over the next year if inflation maintains its downward trajectory. Current market expectations, based on LSEG data, suggest the Bank of England will hold rates steady at its December meeting before implementing three 25-basis-point cuts. Should all four reductions occur, the central bank's key interest rate could drop to around 3.75%, following two rate cuts earlier this year.

Out of power 

The French government collapsed on Wednesday after losing a no-confidence vote, plunging the euro zone's second-largest economy deeper into political turmoil and jeopardizing its ability to legislate and address its fragile finances.

This marks the first time a French government has been ousted by a no-confidence vote since 1962. Prime Minister Michel Barnier will now be required to submit his resignation to President Emmanuel Macron, ending his tenure just three months after taking office on September 5. Barnier’s administration is now the shortest-lived in the history of France’s Fifth Republic, established in 1958.

Going offensive

Brian Thompson, CEO of UnitedHealth’s insurance division, was tragically shot and killed Wednesday morning outside the Hilton hotel in Midtown, according to reports from the NY Post and Bloomberg, citing police sources.

UnitedHealth has not yet responded to a request for comment from Reuters. The incident occurred on the same day the company was hosting its investor day, which was cut short due to "a very serious medical situation" involving a team member, UnitedHealth CEO Andrew Witty stated.

Opening wings

French energy giant TotalEnergies has announced a $1.65 billion acquisition of German renewable developer VSB Group from Swiss asset manager Partners Group. The deal underscores TotalEnergies' commitment to expanding its presence in Germany, a priority market for its integrated power business.

The acquisition will strengthen the company’s capabilities in onshore wind development, aligning with its strategy to grow in renewables. While many oil and gas firms are scaling back their green targets, TotalEnergies continues to buck the trend, expanding its renewable portfolio in markets where it can directly sell power to consumers and capitalise on electricity trading during periods of price volatility.

data.title: Torrent Pharma shares in focus on acquiring 3 anti-diabetes brands
data.description:
Torrent Pharmaceuticals

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Torrent Pharmaceuticals share price will remain in focus on December 5 after the company acquired three brands from Germany's Boehringer Ingelheim GmbH.

"... entered into an agreement to acquire brands Cospiaq (empagliflozin), Cospiaq Met (empagliflozin + metformin) and Xilingio (empagliflozin + linagliptin) from Boehringer Ingelheim International GmbH (BI)," company said in its release.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The acquisition is expected to be completed in March 2025.

Torrent has been marketing these brands since 2022 as part of an existing co-marketing agreement with Boehringer Ingelheim India.

Empagliflozin is a novel sodium glucose co-transporter-2 (SGLT-2) inhibitor which is indicated for glycemic control in adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus.

Catch all the market action on our live blog

The company in the quarter ended September 2024 had posted 40 percent jump in its net profit at Rs 460 crore.

In the month of November, the company received an Establishment Inspection Report (EIR) from USFDA for its facility at Pithampur, Madhya Pradesh.

The share price of Suraj Estate Developers is expected to remain in focus on December 5, following the announcement of its acquisition of a vacant parcel of land in Mahim, Mumbai.

The company, via its wholly-owned subsidiary Iconic Property Developers, has acquired a 1,464-square meter (~15,758 square feet) plot at Senapati Bapat Marg, Mahim (W), Mumbai, for Rs 101 crore.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The company to develop a commercial building with an estimated RERA carpet area of 1.03 lakhs square feet with a gross development value (GDV) of around Rs 525 crore.

Catch all the market action on our live blog

The company had posted 88 percent jump in its Q2FY25 net profit at Rs 31.83 crore, YoY.

The share touched a 52-week high of Rs 842.00 and a 52-week low of Rs 256.00 on 20 August, 2024 and 27 March, 2024, respectively.

Currently, the stock is trading 20.04 percent below its 52-week high and 163.01 percent above its 52-week low.

data.title: First Tick: Here are the top global cues for today’s trade
data.description:
Market Today

Indian benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty 50 are likely to see a flat to negative start on December 5, tracking cues from GIFT Nifty trading around 24,517.50, a short while ago this morning.

Track the latest updates on GIFT Nifty right here on Moneycontrol.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

In a highly volatile session on December 4, the Indian market ended on a positive note and extended the winning streak for fourth straight session with Nifty around 24,450, led by realty and financial stocks.

At close, the Sensex was up 110.58 points or 0.14 percent at 80,956.33, and the Nifty was up 10.30 points or 0.04 percent at 24,467.45.

Here is how financial markets across the globe fared overnight:

GIFT Nifty (Down)

The GIFT Nifty is trading marginally lower, indicating a flat to negative start for the day. Nifty futures were trading at 24,517.50 at 07:00 am IST.
